findings.evidence: `rg` hits: line 264 provides `SemilocallySimplyConnectedAt.exists_pathConnected_slsc_neighborhood`; line 424 has `[SemilocallySimplyConnectedSpace X]`; line 431 uses only `exists_pathConnected_slsc_neighborhood z` for `z` on `Set.range γ`.
findings.fix: State the general theorem with `SemilocallySimplyConnectedOn (Set.range γ)` or `∀ z ∈ Set.range γ, SemilocallySimplyConnectedAt z`, using `SemilocallySimplyConnectedAt.exists_pathConnected_slsc_neighborhood`, and derive the current global-class version as a wrapper if needed.
findings.issue: `Path.exists_partition_in_slsc_neighborhoods` assumes `[SemilocallySimplyConnectedSpace X]`, but the construction only needs SLSC at points of `Set.range γ`; this makes the reusable partition API unnecessarily global.

summary: One public partition theorem is stated with a global SLSC typeclass even though the file already has the pointwise API needed to state the natural local version along the path.
